evals.name: alloy-docker-log-collection
evals.prompt: I'm running Plex, Sonarr, and Radarr on Docker Compose and I want to add log collection so I can search container logs in Grafana. What agent should I use and how do I configure it?
evals.expected_output: Use Alloy (not the Docker Loki log driver) as a separate container in the Compose stack. Configure it with a discovery.docker block pointing at the Docker socket, a discovery.relabel block to extract container and service labels, a loki.source.docker block to collect logs, and a loki.write block targeting http://loki:3100/loki/api/v1/push. Mount /var/run/docker.sock and /var/lib/docker/containers read-only. The Docker Loki log driver should be avoided because it sends logs synchronously and will block container stdout if Loki is unreachable.
expectations: Recommends Alloy, not the Docker Loki log driver
Explains that the Docker Loki log driver blocks container stdout when Loki is unreachable, causing containers to hang
Shows a discovery.docker block with host = "unix:///var/run/docker.sock" in Alloy's .alloy config syntax
Shows a loki.source.docker block forwarding to loki.write.default.receiver
Mounts /var/run/docker.sock and /var/lib/docker/containers read-only into the Alloy container

evals.name: servicemonitor-not-discovered
evals.prompt: I deployed kube-prometheus-stack on my k3s homelab and created a ServiceMonitor for my Sonarr exporter, but Prometheus shows 0 targets and never scrapes it. What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: The default kube-prometheus-stack configuration only discovers ServiceMonitors that carry the same Helm release labels, silently ignoring any you create manually. Fix this by setting serviceMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues: false, podMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues: false, and ruleSelectorNilUsesHelmValues: false under prometheus.prometheusSpec in your Helm values, then upgrading the release. This is the #1 gotcha with kube-prometheus-stack.
expectations: Identifies serviceMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues as the root cause, not a networking or RBAC issue
Specifies all three selector flags: serviceMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues, podMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues, and ruleSelectorNilUsesHelmValues set to false
Places the fix under prometheus.prometheusSpec in Helm values (not at the top level)
Notes that without the fix Prometheus silently ignores manually created ServiceMonitors even though kubectl get servicemonitor shows them
Avoids recommending manual label matching or recreating the ServiceMonitor as the fix

evals.name: alertmanager-discord-email-routing
evals.prompt: I want to set up alerting on my homelab monitoring stack so critical alerts go to Discord and warnings go to email. Show me the Alertmanager config.
evals.expected_output: Configure alertmanager.yml with a route that groups by alertname and job, sets group_wait: 30s, group_interval: 5m, and repeat_interval: 4h, with a default receiver of discord. Add a child route matching severity: critical to the discord receiver and severity: warning to the email receiver. The discord receiver uses discord_configs with a webhook_url (reference $DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL from the environment). The email receiver uses email_configs with smarthost, auth_username, and auth_password. Never commit the webhook URL or SMTP password — use .env file substitution or a Kubernetes Secret mounted via webhook_url_file.
expectations: Sets repeat_interval: 4h (not a generic value like 1h or 24h)
Uses discord_configs under the discord receiver (not a generic webhook_configs block)
Routes severity: critical to discord and severity: warning to email as separate child routes
Keeps the webhook URL and SMTP password out of the config file — references $DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL via env substitution or webhook_url_file for k3s
Includes group_by: ['alertname', 'job'] matching the skill's reference config
